blocks.content: Evil Strikes Back (3)
Imperial City of Aganos.
The goblin's attire sharply contrasted with the corridor's decoration of gold and treasures.
Clad in a ragged cloak and carrying a crude spear on his back, the monster's name was Kido.
He was one of the few royal guards who could deliver food directly to Wuorin's room.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Stop.
A black whirlwind blew in, blocking the path with an owl-like white mask worn by the Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Leader
blocks.content: What? You scared me.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …Where are you going?

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Leader
blocks.content: Can't you see? That woman asked for some honey water.
The Wind Guards, staring intently at the golden tray Kido was holding, revealed his killing intent.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Leader
blocks.content: Don't get cocky just because the Empress dotes on you. At the end of the day, you're just a pet.
Like most of his close associates, for the Wind Guards, the sole ruler of Kashan was Terra.
These masters of the sword, who embodied fluid dynamics in their bodies, protected Terraje up close.
However, since Kido entered the Imperial Palace, that pride had been shattered.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: 'Just a goblin.'
From what he observed from afar, Wuorin didn't truly value Kido either.
She treated him like a servant, sometimes angrily, and occasionally took him for a walk on a leash.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: If I'm a pet, what are you guys? Cattle? Move aside. If I'm late, that woman will throw a fit.
The Wind Guards activated the collective killing intent 'Gaksi,' feeling like thousands of blades were pricking his skin.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Leader
blocks.content: Watch your words. If you insult the Empress again, I'll report you myself. That stinking goblin will be sent to a place he can never return from.
The biggest reason the Wind Guards despised Kido was that he harbored affection for Wuorin.
'A dog looking to surpass its master?' Wuorin wouldn't have kept him around if it weren't for his unique ability, 'The Taste of Memory.'

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Move aside.
Kido had no intention to fight.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I just had a good idea.
As the leader of the squadron drew his sword, the remaining nine made the same motion, like afterimages.

blocks.speaker: Wind Guard Leader
blocks.content: Let's neuter you. Then the Empress can play with you much more safely.
The Wind Guards was serious.
Kido's eyes turned cold, but his hands still clutched the tray.
A subtle vibration that appears before the body moves heightened the tension.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Stop.
A woman's voice was heard.
Lady Yula.
The Wind Guards sheathed his sword and bowed his head, while Kido slowly turned around.
'When did she get behind me...'
The leader of the Wind Guards, Yula.
Wearing the same owl mask, but her aura was different from the other Wind Guards.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: The Wind Guards obeys only the Empress's commands. Remember that your arrogance could destroy the entire organization.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I apologize.
Yula's chest heaved deeply then settled.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: 'This is why newcomers are a problem. No matter how much you teach them with words, they don't understand until they experience it.'
As capable as a squadron leader might be, all the Wind Guards must remember this.
'Terraje.'
'Just because she finds someone displeasing, she could annihilate all of the Wind Guards.'
Yula bowed her head.

blocks.speaker: Yula
blocks.content: I'm sorry. Overlook my subordinate's rudeness. The Empress is waiting, hurry on.
When Kido turned around, the corridor was empty.
Since Yula would not be there anyway, he did not bother to look back and continued walking.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: …It's not easy.
Kido knocked on Wuorin's door, but there was no response.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: I'm entering.
Kido lifted his right foot to turn the doorknob while holding the tray with both hands.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: What, you were here?
Wuorin, who had finished grooming herself, was peering outside from a table next to the window.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Kido.
She turned her head with a smile, her appearance shining brighter than the backlight from the window.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: 'She's really beautiful.'
Although goblin aesthetic standards differ from humans, to his eyes, Wuorin was perfect.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: 'Maybe because she's eaten many people. Human memories have seeped into her.'

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He tried to divert his thoughts, but the truth was clear.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: 'I love this woman.'
All the desires to laugh together, walk together, talk about the book they read yesterday, and entangle like animals.
If someone finds such natural desires repulsive, there's only one reason.
Kido is a goblin.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Drink it. You said your stomach was upset?
Looking at the teacup shaped like a water droplet on the tray, Wuorin's smile deepened.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Yap.
She hit under the tray with her hand, causing the teacup to topple and spill all the honey water.
Hiding her face to contain her anger, Wuorin held her belly and laughed.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Hahaha! Look at that face.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What are you doing?

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Huh? I hit the tray so the honey water spilled. Oh, right. Could you get some more honey water? My stomach is upset.
Throwing the tray aside, Kido yelled.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: What do you think goblins are! Am I a joke to you? Why is it always a joke when you see me?

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: It's disgusting.
As Kido's face turned pale with shock, Wuorin laughed sharply.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: It's a joke, a joke! How much I like you.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Enough.
Kido turned around.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: If you need a pet, find someone else. I'm quitting. I'm leaving Kashan.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Why are you so sensitive today? What happened?

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: 'No, this is my problem.'

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: He couldn't bear the miserable sight of himself the more he stayed by her side.

blocks.speaker: Kido
blocks.content: Huh? Are you really going? Kido, Kido!
Before Wuorin could finish speaking, Kido had flung open the door and escaped into the corridor.
As the door slammed shut, the room returned to silence as before.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Hmph, that's all he was.
Whether he could really leave Kashan was uncertain, but at least he needed time to think.
As Kido left the Imperial Palace.
Wuorin, sitting at the table looking out the window, had eyes that had grown cold.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Be careful, Kido.
In the indifferent landscape she viewed, corpses bleeding profusely were emerging.
The face of Harvitz, who had arrived in the capital of Kashan, was bloodied like a demon.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Thrilling.
He entered the construction site to repair the damage caused by the demonic tree, but no one recognized him.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Be more terrified.
Every time Wuorin's inner voice was heard, his eyelids trembled.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Hey, get the bricks from the storage...
As Harvitz swung his arm, the neck of a passing worker snapped.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Come on! Let's start quickly!
People remained busy, and Harvitz, alone, began his deadly dance.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Die.
One life per slash.

blocks.speaker: Wuorin
blocks.content: Die.
Even as he focused on that tedious task, the city moved rapidly.
The teachers, who had expected a terrible clash, were stunned the moment they opened their eyes.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Oh my…
Wizard was crying.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I won't do it again.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Yes. Only those with courage can admit their mistakes. It's something even adults find difficult.
Wizard, having stopped crying, sniffled.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Winning and hurting someone are completely different things. You must not do that in the future.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'A genius of the era, a magician stronger than adults, intoxicated with all sorts of flowery words, but…'
In reality, the child is anxious about everything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Their own emotions, others' emotions, even the nameless insects crawling at their feet…'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Everything is scary.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'No one has taught this child about the world. Thanks to that, their purity is maintained, but…'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'That's why it's difficult.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Am I really going to send this child to Satan? Can I tell them to kill Havitz?'
Shirone, sorting out his complicated feelings, took Wizard's hand and left the training ground.
While doing so, Wizard, lost in thought, looked up at the teachers and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Teacher, I'm sorry. I apologize.
The dedicated teacher, with a tearful expression, gently stroked her cheek.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: No, Wizard did really well. You were wonderful. I'm so proud of you.
Lampa, who had been watching warmly, turned his head and brought up the main topic.

blocks.speaker: Lampa
blocks.content: Then shall we… set up the negotiation table?
Though not outwardly visible, the administrator's heart was boiling with anger.

blocks.speaker: Administrator
blocks.content: 'Damn it! This is the worst situation.'

blocks.speaker: Administrator
blocks.content: Who would have thought that the untamable Wizard would become a docile lamb?

blocks.speaker: Lampa
blocks.content: Haha! Yes. Shall we return to the school? Wizard's parents are waiting.
Leaving the teachers behind, they entered the school, and the principal approached with Wizard's parents.

blocks.speaker: Principal
blocks.content: Welcome. I am Nikolai, a certified 4th-grade magician. It's an honor to meet the star of the Ivory Tower.
To magicians, Shirone was a living legend.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, nice to meet you. I've heard of your fame.
Through Omega.

blocks.speaker: Principal
blocks.content: You flatter me. Ah, and these are Wizard's parents. Please introduce yourselves.
A man and woman in their mid-forties bowed their heads.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: Hello. I'm Wizard's father. If my daughter has been rude, please forgive her…
Seeing him speak without even looking, it was easy to guess Wizard's usual behavior.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, we are the ones receiving help. Thank you for raising your daughter well.
It was a statement representing humanity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'd like to hear about it. How Wizard was born and what phenomena occurred.
The mother, flustered, replied.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: We're just farmers, so we don't know well. What is it, magic or Spirit Zone…

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Oh, yes, that. We just want Wizard to be safe. In a world where demons run rampant…
The mother wiped away her tears.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He couldn't bring himself to say that her daughter would fight the leader of those demons.
Mom! Dad!
At that moment, Wizard, who had just washed up, arrived and ran to the entrance, waving her hand.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Wizard!
The child, who threw herself into her parents' arms, was unmistakably seven years old, making Shirone's heart even heavier.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Even if all conditions are met, it's useless if Wizard doesn't do it.'

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: This brother, he's really strong. Much, much stronger than me.
The parents, who had never seen Wizard acknowledge anyone, were dumbfounded.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I like this brother. I want to keep playing with him. Is that okay? Yes? Yes?
The parents looked at Shirone again.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: It's the first time our daughter has acted like this. May I ask your name?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I am Shirone.
They had never heard of the Ivory Tower, but the name Shirone was famous even among farmers.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Thank you. Thanks to you, Shirone, we were able to escape safely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I don't deserve such words. And I won't in the future either.
Leaving the confused parents behind, Shirone met Wizard's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wizard, do you still find magic fun?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Yes, it's fun. It's more fun playing with you, brother. I want to keep doing magic. Forever and ever!
Wizard, pouting her lips like a fish, pointed her index finger and drew a long line to the side.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: You could die.
At Shirone's words, the parents' eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Excuse me! What are you saying right now?
As the parents tried to intervene, Lampa put his finger to his lips and shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I could die…
After thinking for a long time, she suddenly looked up as if she had realized something.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Then, looking up at Shirone, she answered with the brightest smile a seven-year-old could give.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Yes! I want to do it!
